summary: Keep the M3 Welcome dialog enabled for fresh users while making pre-Welcome browser acceptance journeys explicitly close it before interacting with the workbench.

decisions.decision: Legacy workbench acceptance journeys close the Welcome dialog through the same visible Close welcome control a user has, without setting the suppression preference or changing production defaults. A dedicated fresh-profile browser journey independently requires the dialog and opens an Appendix F sample.
decisions.rationale: The Welcome screen is required production behavior, while older browser journeys intentionally begin at the workbench. Explicitly closing the dialog preserves both contracts and avoids contaminating localStorage assertions or masking a Welcome regression with global test state.

testEvidence.criterion: The Welcome dialog remains enabled while all pre-Welcome browser acceptance journeys can reach the workbench
testEvidence.test: Run the affected Playwright files serially, including a dedicated fresh-profile Welcome sample journey.

red.command: GitHub Actions run 29458260074: Browser acceptance (ubuntu-latest) and Browser acceptance (windows-latest)
red.outcome: RED: both operating-system jobs reported the same deterministic result, 11 failed and 9 passed. Playwright identified the visible Welcome to ScadMill dialog as intercepting pointer events in legacy journeys.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec playwright test tests/e2e/c14-welcome.e2e.ts tests/e2e/c0-responsive.e2e.ts tests/e2e/c9-settings-layout.e2e.ts tests/e2e/m2-browser-gate.e2e.ts tests/e2e/m2-storage-fallback.e2e.ts tests/e2e/m2-workspace-onboarding.e2e.ts --workers=1
green.outcome: GREEN: 17 tests passed in 39.1 seconds with one worker.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
